events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess a residential electrical job: Receptacles & switches, observed condition "reversed polarity" at rough-complete stage. We need to decide compliance, risk, etc. Reversed polarity means hot and neutral are swapped. This is a safety hazard and violates NEC 406.4 (receptacle installation) and possibly 210.8 (GFCI protection) but reversed polarity is a direct violation. It's non-compliant and defective. Decision: fail. Risk: high (shock hazard). Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80%? Rough-in done but not finished. Value: none. Sound source: none. Confidence: high. Event time, rate: not applicable. Order: none. Workable: maybe none, but we can list corrective actions. Findings: reversed polarity. Actions: correct wiring. Rationale: reversed polarity creates shock hazard, violates NEC 406.4. References: NEC 406.4. So output JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Reversed polarity at receptacle: hot and neutral conductors swapped."],
  "actions": ["Immediately de-energize circuit and correct wiring to match terminal designations (brass to hot, silver to neutral)."],
  "rationale": "Reversed polarity creates a shock hazard even when the switch is off, violates NEC 406.4 requirement for proper connection of receptacle terminals.",
  "references": ["NEC 406.4"]
}
